channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 📍 Blue Origin rocket explosion shows ‘fragility’ of national-security launch plans | Defense One
item.description: Space Force efforts to breed more competitors aren’t keeping up with ever-increasing demand for rockets.

item.title: 🕵️ GOP lawmakers axe provision to limit the use of JAGs in civilian roles | Defense One
item.description: The Trump administration has shifted dozens of military lawyers to temporary jobs as immigration judges, special attorneys.

item.title: 🏴 Putin allies wishcast nuclear war, America in crisis, and real-estate deals | Defense One
item.description: On Wednesday, they met at the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum as smoke from Ukrainian drone attacks rose across the city.

item.title: 🏴 House passes Ukraine aid bill with new sanctions for Russia | Breaking Defense
item.description: House members voted 226-195 to pass the Ukraine Support Act, which authorizes $8 billion in military finance loans to Ukraine and extends the Ukraine Security Assistance Initiative through 2027.

item.title: 🌏 Philippines’ UN loss strengthens case for minilateralism | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The UN General Assembly has just strengthened the case for minilateralism, the gathering of small coalitions to act when international institutions fail. On 3 June it had a chance to do what we argued for ...

item.title: 📍 USAF Wants “MQ-9 Next” Reaper Replacement To Be Modular, Cheap | The War Zone
item.description: Concerning MQ-9 losses have turbocharged discussions about a successor to the venerable Reaper. The post USAF Wants “MQ-9 Next” Reaper Replacement To Be Modular, Cheap appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 Kushner-Linked Luxury Resort Plans Ignite Protests Across Albania | Foreign Policy
item.description: Thousands call for Tirana to block the investment projects, citing environmental and corruption concerns.

item.title: 📍 Meanwhile, in the Western Hemisphere… | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. military remains extremely active in and around Latin America.

item.title: 🌍 How Trump Should Approach an Iran Endgame | Foreign Policy
item.description: Former Iran negotiator Robert Malley on the White House’s best options right now.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine just showed the whole world that Putin is losing control of the war | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine's audacious drone strike on St. Petersburg as the Russian city hosted a flagship international economic forum known as "Putin's Davos" sent a clear signal to global audiences that the Kremlin dictator is losing control of the war, writes Peter Dickinson. The post Ukraine…

item.title: 📍 تفتت الفصائل المسلحة العراقية بعد تحول النظام الإيراني من الثيوقراطية الى الجنتوقراطية | Atlantic Council
item.description: كيف يؤثر تغيير القيادة في ايران على الميليشيات العراقية؟ د. منقذ داغر يبدو اننا بتنا ، بعد 28 فبراير 2026 ،امام نظام مختلف وظيفياً، بل وحتى هيكلياً عما عرفناه طوال السنوات السبعة وأربعين من عمر النظام الايراني. فقد اتسم نظام الحكم في ايران بنسخته الخمينية الأولى 1979-1989…

item.title: 🏛️ Anti-Drone 5.56mm Rifle Rounds That Break Into Multiple Projectiles Sought By Marines | The War Zone
item.description: The aim is for specialized rounds to help boost the ability of any Marine with a rifle to swat down small uncrewed attackers. The post Anti-Drone 5.56mm Rifle Rounds That Break Into Multiple Projectiles Sought By Marines appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 Trump Is Playing With Fire in Cuba | Foreign Policy
item.description: The U.S. blockade on the island is unprecedented—and could lead to prolonged upheaval.

item.title: 🏴 Europe cannot afford to leave Ukraine trapped in the geopolitical grey zone | Atlantic Council
item.description: Europe cannot afford to leave Ukraine stuck in the geopolitical grey zone as this uncertainty fuels Russia's invasion and feeds Putin's dreams of a new Russian Empire, write Maksym Beznosiuk and William Dixon. The post Europe cannot afford to leave Ukraine trapped in the…

item.title: 🌏 Rising Security Risks Are Changing China’s Belt and Road Strategy | The Diplomat
item.description: Beijing is becoming more selective about where and what it builds overseas. That transition was already underway but it has been hastened by the Iran-U.S. war.

item.title: 📍 From crisis partners to strategic partners: deepening Australia–UAE defence ties | ASPI Strategist
item.description: The time is right for Australia and the United Arab Emirates to deepen their defence relationship. For both countries it would be one of the most substantial partnerships outside existing formal alliances. Momentum has been ...

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine Tests New Missile In Hopes Of Leading To Low Cost Patriot Alternative | The War Zone
item.description: As interceptor shortages strain its defenses, Ukraine has a plan to build anti-ballistic missiles that are cheaper and faster to produce than Western types. The post Ukraine Tests New Missile In Hopes Of Leading To Low Cost Patriot Alternative appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌏 Did China Overestimate the Geopolitical Returns of Its Latin America Strategy? | The Diplomat
item.description: The assumption that economic leverage leads to political influence has underpinned China analysis for decades. Developments in Latin America are calling that into question.

item.title: 🔐 Cyber Innovation Warfare center will pair industry side-by-side with operators | Breaking Defense
item.description: Elements of the concept are already operational today.

item.title: 📍 Amendment to eliminate funds for Trump-class battleship falters in HASC defense bill markup | Breaking Defense
item.description: “Whatever the number is [for the BBG(X) program], I’m going to give you right one of the surest fire Kalshi bets you will ever have: Take the over,” said Rep. Adam Smith, D-Wash.

item.title: 📍 Is This A Glimpse Of The Aircraft That Gave Birth To The F-47? | The War Zone
item.description: A purported sighting over Area 51 has features that would fit with what we think we know about the F-47, but many questions remain. The post Is This A Glimpse Of The Aircraft That Gave Birth To The F-47? appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 📍 What Shangri-La 2026 Revealed About the Future Regional Order | The Diplomat
item.description: Like the CPTPP after the U.S. exit from TPP, the next phase of regional security may be shaped by what U.S. allies and partners build when Washington moves one step back.

item.title: 🌏 China Is Too Big for Shangri-La | Foreign Policy
item.description: Snubbing a key security forum shows Beijing’s new confidence.

item.title: 🌏 New DPRK-Russia Friendship Hospital is Repurposing Existing Project | 38 North
item.description: In late April, when officials from Russia and North Korea broke ground on the new DPRK-Russia ... The post New DPRK-Russia Friendship Hospital is Repurposing Existing Project appeared first on 38 North.

item.title: 📍 Brazil plans to buy 20 more Gripen fighters | Breaking Defense
item.description: Gen. Marcelo Kanitz Damasceno, the Brazilian air chief, told Breaking Defense that the need for extra jets was born from a serious look at Brazil’s air power.

item.title: 📍 Trump’s New ‘Forced Labor’ Tariffs Are a Fig Leaf | Foreign Policy
item.description: The new import duties are “a solution in search of a problem.”

item.title: 🌏 Is Time on China’s Side? Beijing’s Taiwan Calculus and the Balance of Power | War on the Rocks
item.description: When is the risk of war the highest? And what should the United States be doing about it? One of the most important but underappreciated questions in international politics is how states think about the future balance of power. Countries that believe their position is improving…

item.title: 📍 Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions | Just Security
item.description: A public resource tracking all the legal challenges to the Trump administration's executive orders and actions. The post Litigation Tracker: Legal Challenges to Trump Administration Actions appeared first on Just Security.

item.title: 📍 A US oil export ban could raise pump prices | Atlantic Council
item.description: If the Strait of Hormuz closure continues and gasoline prices increase further, US policymakers might consider banning oil exports. But instead of lowering prices, it could have the opposite effect. The post A US oil export ban could raise pump prices appeared first on Atlantic…

item.title: 🏛️ Pentagon’s Cyber Defense Command drafting plan to defend critical infrastructure | Breaking Defense
item.description: DoD Cyber Defense Command is working to develop a joint task force for defense of critical infrastructure.
